_about: Canonical source of truth for the federal benefit programs Alara accepts. Every surface (home.html, programs.html, white-card.html, learn.html, qualify.html, and the server) reads facts from here instead of hand-copying them. Promoted and reconciled from content/_examples/programs.json. When a fact changes, change it HERE.

constants.costPhraseLong: no premiums, copays, or deductibles for covered care tied to the accepted condition
constants._costNote: CANONICAL. 'no cost to you' is the truthful, approved phrasing (the federal program pays in full, not just out-of-pocket). Do NOT use 'pay nothing', 'most patients pay nothing', or '$0 out-of-pocket' — those read cheap and were retired.

dolResourceCenter.label: DOL Las Vegas Resource Center
dolResourceCenter.role: No-cost navigation for Test Site workers in Southern Nevada: filing help, benefit questions, provider connections.

acentra.label: Acentra (DOL billing administrator)
acentra.role: Answers EEOICPA benefit and coverage questions directly.

programs.name: White Card
programs.fullName: Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act
programs.audience: Former Nevada Test Site, Nevada National Security Site, Department of Energy, and Atomic Weapons Employer workers, and eligible survivors, with an accepted EEOICPA condition.
programs.shortDefinition: A federal program that provides cash compensation and lifetime medical benefits — including skilled home health — to Department of Energy and Nevada Test Site workers who became ill from their work. An accepted condition issues a White Card.

cost.summary: Generally no cost to you for covered care tied to the accepted condition.
cost.detail: No premiums, copays, or deductibles for those covered services. The Department of Labor pays the provider directly.

missedBenefits.title: Travel reimbursement (form OWCP-957)
missedBenefits.body: Every trip to a doctor, specialist, hospital, lab, or pharmacy for an accepted condition is reimbursable at the federal mileage rate. A patient going to oncology twice a week and the pharmacy weekly can leave hundreds of dollars a month unclaimed.

action.label: Do this yourself
action.text: Download form OWCP-957 from dol.gov, log your mileage or attach receipts, and submit it to DEEOIC. They mail you a check. You do not need us to do this, and you can claim past trips.

missedBenefits.title: A family member can be paid to provide your care
missedBenefits.body: If a spouse, son, or daughter already helps you bathe, dress, cook, or get to appointments, they may be employed and paid for that care through your White Card. They become a trained, nurse-supervised home health aide, and the program covers their wages.

action.label: How to start
action.text: This one runs through an agency. Ask Alara about enrolling your family member when you set up care. One rule: that family member cannot also serve as your Authorized Representative with the DOL.

missedBenefits.title: You may be paying Medicare copays the White Card should cover
missedBenefits.body: Many patients run their cancer treatment, medications, or equipment through Medicare and pay copays, when the White Card should cover those, related to an accepted condition, at no cost. A chemotherapy support drug at $200 a month, or a hospital bed at $150 a month, may already be covered.

action.label: Check this
action.text: Ask whether your treatment, medications, and equipment for your accepted condition are billed through your White Card. If you are paying out of pocket for anything related to it, that is usually fixable.

missedBenefits.title: An updated impairment rating
missedBenefits.body: The DOL pays additional lump-sum compensation based on a physician's impairment rating. If your condition has worsened since your original claim, a re-evaluation may entitle you to more.

action.label: Who handles it
action.text: Your Authorized Representative files impairment requests. Ask them whether a re-evaluation is worth doing. (Alara does not file these.)

missedBenefits.title: Survivor benefits
missedBenefits.body: When a covered worker dies from an accepted condition, the surviving spouse and eligible children may be entitled to compensation. Many widows of Test Site workers never learn this.

action.label: Who handles it
action.text: An Authorized Representative can determine eligibility and file. If you are not sure whether you have one, the Resource Center can point you.

does: Provide the home health care your card covers
Handle the authorization and the billing
Screen for and pursue consequential conditions
Identify benefits you are missing, and educate you on them
Connect you to your Resource Center and Authorized Representative
doesNot: File your initial EE-1 or EE-2 claim
Act as your Authorized Representative with the DOL
Take any percentage of your compensation

programs.name: OWCP / FECA
programs.fullName: Office of Workers' Compensation Programs (Federal Employees' Compensation Act)
programs.audience: Federal and postal (USPS) workers with an OWCP-accepted, work-related injury or illness, with care prescribed by a physician and authorized by OWCP.
programs.shortDefinition: Federal workers' compensation that may cover physician-prescribed home health services when an injury or illness is accepted and care is authorized.

cost.summary: Generally no cost to you for authorized care tied to the accepted injury or illness.
cost.detail: No premiums, copays, or deductibles for those authorized services.

programs.name: VA Community Care
programs.audience: VA-enrolled veterans with a referral or authorization and a provider order. Alara is a TriWest provider for Region 4.
programs.shortDefinition: A VA program that authorizes eligible veterans to receive skilled home health from approved community providers when VA facilities cannot readily provide the care.

cost.summary: Generally no cost to you for authorized community care.
cost.detail: Covered through the VA Community Care Network on authorization.
